name: C011.2: ||psi||^2 is real and positive (Hilbert space norm)
C011.3b: Hermitian operator has real eigenvalues
C011.inv: Linearity preserved (A(α|a>+β|b>) = αA|a>+βA|b>)
detail: Operator linearity is structural invariant of the chain

name: C011.trans: Step labels correct
detail: 1→2: EXTEND (add inner product). 2→3: REPRESENT (operators on Hilbert space). 3→4: REDUCE (extract scalar expectation). All consistent.

name: C012.1: Laplacian L = D - A constructed
C012.inv: Row sums of Laplacian = 0 (conservation law)
C012.2: Laplacian is positive semidefinite (smallest eigenval = 0)
C012.3a: Heat kernel at t=0 is identity
C012.3b: Constant vector in kernel of L (L*1 = 0)
C012.4: Eigenvalue structure gives diffusion timescales
detail: Eigenvalues: [0, 3]. Mode 0 = steady state, mode 3 = fastest decay.

name: C012.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: MAP (A,D → L=D-A). 2→3: MAP (matrix exponential). 3→4: REPRESENT (dynamics from spectral decomposition). Consistent.

name: C013.2: Euler-Lagrange for geodesic: ∂F/∂y' = y'/√(1+y'²)
detail: ∂F/∂y' = yp/sqrt(yp**2 + 1), which is constant → y'' = 0 → straight line

name: C013.3: Lagrange multipliers find constrained extrema
C013.inv: Extremization preserved across all steps
detail: Finite ∇f=0 → functional δJ=0 → EL equation → constrained EL. All express 'stationarity' in progressively richer spaces.

name: C013.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: EXTEND (finite→infinite dim). 2→3: REDUCE (derive necessary condition). 3→4: EXTEND (add constraint structure). Consistent.

name: C014.2: Representation preserves multiplication (M_s * M_r = M_{sr})
C014.3a: S3 has 3 irreducible representations
detail: Trivial (dim 1), sign (dim 1), standard (dim 2). Sum of squares: 1² + 1² + 2² = 6 = |S3|. Verified.

name: C014.3b: Sum of squares of irrep dimensions = |G|
C014.4: Characters are traces of representation matrices
C014.inv: Symmetry structure preserved through representation
detail: Homomorphism G → GL(V) preserves group multiplication. Characters are class functions (constant on conjugacy classes).

name: C014.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: REPRESENT (abstract group → matrices). 2→3: REDUCE (decompose into irreducibles). 3→4: MAP (representation → scalar trace). Consistent.

name: C015.4: β₁ = 1 for hollow triangle (one cycle/hole)
detail: Without the face, the cycle e01+e12-e02 is not a boundary → 1-hole

name: C015.inv: Betti numbers are topological invariants
detail: β_n depends only on topology, not triangulation. Verified via linear algebra of boundary operators. NOTE: Full topological invariance proof requires algebraic topology (simplicial approximation theorem), not just SymPy.

name: C015.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: DISCRETIZE (space → simplicial complex). 2→3: REDUCE (quotient ker/im). 3→4: REDUCE (rank extraction). Consistent.

name: C016.1: Metric tensor for S² constructed
C016.4: Einstein tensor vanishes in 2D (known identity)
C016.inv: Coordinate invariance (diffeomorphism invariance)
detail: All geometric objects (g, Γ, R, G) transform covariantly under coord changes. Ricci scalar R = 2/r² is a scalar invariant — same in all coordinates.

name: C016.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: EXTEND (add metric structure). 2→3: DERIVE (curvature from metric). 3→4: COUPLE (geometry = matter via field equation). Consistent.

name: C017.4: Sequential update — second posterior normalizes
C017.inv: Probability consistency (normalization preserved at each step)
detail: Every posterior integrates to 1. Sequential update preserves normalization.

name: C017.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: EXTEND (add prior belief). 2→3: REDUCE (normalize). 3→4: ITERATE (posterior→prior cycle). Consistent.

name: C018.1: Q is a field
detail: QQ in SymPy represents the rationals. Closed under +,-,*,/ (nonzero). Verified by construction.

name: C018.2a: Minimal polynomial of √2 over Q is x²-2
detail: x² - 2 is irreducible over Q: True

name: C018.3: Galois group of x²-2 is S2 ≅ Z/2Z
C018.4: Fundamental theorem — subgroup-field correspondence
detail: Z/2Z has 2 subgroups → 2 fields: Q ↔ Z/2Z, Q(√2) ↔ {e}. Lattice of subgroups is anti-isomorphic to lattice of intermediate fields.

name: C018.5: Galois group of x⁴-2 is D4 (order 8)
detail: Gal(splitting field of x⁴-2) = (<S4TransitiveSubgroups.D4: 'D4'>, False). Rich subgroup lattice demonstrating fundamental theorem.

name: C018.inv: Algebraic structure preserved
detail: Field axioms hold in Q and all extensions. Galois group encodes the symmetries of the extension.

name: C018.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: EXTEND (adjoin root). 2→3: MAP (field automorphisms). 3→4: DUALIZE (subgroup ↔ subfield anti-isomorphism). Consistent.

name: C019.1: -u'' = λu satisfied by sin(nx) with λ = n²
C019.3: Operator -d²/dx² is self-adjoint (with Dirichlet BCs)
C019.4: Spectral solution -u'' = sin(3x) → u = sin(3x)/9
C019.inv: Linear structure preserved
detail: PDE, weak form, operator equation, spectral decomposition all preserve linearity. Superposition holds at every step.

name: C019.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: EXTEND (pointwise → distributional/weak). 2→3: MAP (function → operator theory). 3→4: DECOMPOSE (spectral expansion). Consistent.

name: C020.1: Logistic map f(x) = rx(1-x) defined
C020.2a: Fixed points of logistic map
C020.2b: Stability derivative at x* = 1-1/r is 2-r
detail: f'(x*) = 2 - r. Stable when |2-r| < 1, i.e., 1 < r < 3.

name: C020.3a: Lyapunov exponent at r=4 ≈ ln(2) ≈ 0.693
C020.3b: Lyapunov exponent at r=2.5 < 0 (stable/non-chaotic)
detail: λ(r=2.5) = -0.693127 < 0 → stable fixed point, no chaos

name: C020.3c: Lyapunov exponent at r=3.57 ≈ 0 (edge of chaos)
C020.4: Sensitivity to initial conditions at r=4
detail: After 50 iterations, Δx₀ = 1e-10 grew to Δx₅₀ = 0.356617. Exponential divergence = chaos.

name: C020.5: Period-doubling bifurcation at r=3
detail: At r=3: |f'(x*)| = |2-3| = 1 → eigenvalue crosses unit circle → period-2 orbit born. Start of Feigenbaum cascade.

name: C020.inv: State space preserved, predictability destroyed
detail: x ∈ [0,1] throughout (state space invariant). Deterministic dynamics preserved. Predictability destroyed: positive Lyapunov exponent → SDIC.

name: C020.trans: Transformation types correct
detail: 1→2: ITERATE (apply map repeatedly). 2→3: REDUCE (extract exponent from trajectory). 3→4: THRESHOLD (λ > 0 → qualitative change). Consistent.
